title: Ari Shapiro: 'How to Fix Capitalism'
summary: Journalist, author, and former NPR host Ari Shapiro devotes his entire Substack newsletter to Incorruptible, opening with a reference to the Norwegian Consumer Council's viral video about 'enshittification' before diving into why Ries's book is different from anything else he's read on corporate dysfunction. Shapiro walks through the central argument — that companies like Costco, Patagonia, and Duolingo aren't just good corporate citizens, they actually outperform their competitors financially — and contrasts it with cautionary tales of Boeing, Whole Foods, and Polaroid. He highlights the Bombas case study, where founders David Heath and Randy Goldberg built a $3.4 billion company while donating over 200 million pairs of socks. What makes Shapiro's review distinctive is his framing of Ries's argument as 'the call coming from inside the house' — unlike distant policy proposals, this is a founder and CEO explaining how to fix capitalism from within.
blockquote: Reading it made me so hopeful and excited that I've been telling everyone I know about it. Unlike other solutions that feel distant and slightly unrealistic, this is the call coming from inside the house.
